title: excoecaria agallocha var. camettia
intro: Excoecaria agallocha var. camettia, also known as the Coast Cancana or Poison Tree, is a toxic plant native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and Australia, characterized by its dark green leaves and milky sap. The milky sap of this plant contains a compound called phorbol, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria agallocha var. camettia include wound healing, skin conditions, and even potential anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in some traditional medicine products, but it is essential to use caution and follow proper preparation and dosage guidelines due to its toxicity.
